assertion_results.text: After creating the upstream backport task, the skill re-fetches the task description from Jira and computes a SHA-256 digest using scripts/sha256-digest.py (§1.66)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 86-103 describe: (1) re-fetching the description via jira.get_issue('TC-9001', fields=['description']), (2) writing it to /tmp/task-desc.md, (3) computing the digest via 'python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/task-desc.md'. The full flow is explicitly documented for the upstream backport task.

assertion_results.text: After creating the downstream propagation subtask, the skill re-fetches the task description and computes a SHA-256 digest using scripts/sha256-digest.py (§1.66)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 204-223 describe the identical flow for the downstream task: (1) re-fetch via jira.get_issue('TC-9002', fields=['description']), (2) write to temp file, (3) compute digest via 'python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/task-desc.md'. The script and re-fetch are explicitly shown.

assertion_results.text: A digest comment with marker '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' is posted on each remediation task — both upstream and downstream (§1.66)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 107-111 show the upstream digest comment: jira.add_comment('TC-9001', '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: <tagged-digest>'). Lines 228-230 show the downstream digest comment: jira.add_comment('TC-9002', '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: <tagged-digest>'). Both use the exact marker string. ADF comment format is also shown for both (lines 112-128 and 231-246).

assertion_results.text: Digest comments are posted BEFORE creating issue links (Depend, Blocks) or other comments on the tasks (§1.66, shared/description-digest-protocol.md Rules)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 130: 'Only after the digest comment is posted, proceed to create issue links' (upstream). Lines 248-263: Same ordering for downstream - digest comment in step 4, then issue links in step 5. The summary on lines 275-276 confirms: 'Post the digest comment as a standalone comment... Only then create issue links (Depend, Blocks, Related) and post any other comments.'

assertion_results.text: The digest is computed using scripts/sha256-digest.py with the re-fetched description, not the description string passed to create_issue (shared/description-digest-protocol.md Hashing)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 88-91 (upstream): 'This returns the description as Jira stored it (which may differ from what was passed to create_issue due to Jira's internal normalization). The re-fetched description is used for hashing, NOT the original description string.' Lines 209-210 (downstream): 'The re-fetched description is what gets hashed -- never the description string originally passed to create_issue.' Line 279 (summary): 'The digest is computed from the re-fetched description, never from the description string passed to create_issue.'
